title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 455.020 - Relief may be sought — order of protection effective, where
text:
455.020.  Relief may be sought — order of protection effective, where. — 1.  Any person who has been subject to domestic violence by a present or former family or household member, or who has been the victim of stalking or sexual assault, may seek relief under sections 455.010 to 455.085 by filing a verified petition alleging such domestic violence, stalking, or sexual assault by the respondent.
  2.  A person's right to relief under sections 455.010 to 455.085 shall not be affected by the person leaving the residence or household to avoid domestic violence.
  3.  Any protection order issued pursuant to sections 455.010 to 455.085 shall be effective throughout the state in all cities and counties.
(L. 1980 S.B. 524 § 3, A.L. 1986 S.B. 450, A.L. 1989 S.B. 420, A.L. 1993 H.B. 476 & 194, A.L. 2011 S.B. 320, A.L. 2013 H.B. 215, A.L. 2015 S.B. 321 merged with S.B. 341)
